exemplar.title: How do the LGBT+ free zones signed by 100 Polish municipalities created by an NGO âOrdo Lurisâ correspond to the European Unionâs values and principles?

detailedExemplarData.title: How do the LGBT+ free zones signed by 100 Polish municipalities created by an NGO âOrdo Lurisâ correspond to the European Unionâs values and principles?

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with clear contextualisation of LGBT-free zones within EU values and human rights.
The research question is clearly stated and guides the investigation, maintaining focus on normative correspondence.
A broad range of relevant sources is used, demonstrating purposeful collection even where explicit methodological reflection is limited.
weaknesses: Explanation occasionally lacks precision (e.g. conflating Ordo Iuris with local authorities) and some parts of the narrative drift from the central question.
No dedicated methodology section is present despite being listed in the TOC, limiting transparency of source selection and research design.
Connections between theoretical frameworks and the research question are not fully articulated, reducing methodological clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates accurate and effective communication of the topic and a sharply stated research question, supported by a generally coherent methodology. However, lapses in precision, absence of an explicit methodology section, and only partial integration of theory prevent a topâband score. A 5/6 reflects strong overall performance in Focus and Method while acknowledging these shortcomings.

strengths: Source material (EU legal texts, NGO reports) is clearly relevant and appropriately applied to illustrate PolandâEU tensions.
Demonstrates clear and coherent knowledge of EU humanârights architecture and key IR theories, correctly citing Articles 13, 19, and Charter Article 21.
Terminology such as âsovereignty,â âsoft power,â and âinterdependenceâ is used accurately and consistently to support the argument.
weaknesses: Reliance on journalistic pieces and YouTube explainers dilutes academic rigour; some applications of sources remain descriptive rather than analytical.
Occasional inconsistencies in factual precision (e.g. misnaming Ordo Iuris) and superficial treatment of certain theories (queer theory).
Source integration sometimes lacks synthesis, preventing fully nuanced deployment of material at the highest level.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows clear, coherent understanding of the topic and an effective, mostly accurate use of relevant source material and terminology. Minor precision errors and uneven depth keep it just below the top band, meriting a 5/6.

strengths: Substantial and varied evidence collection from legal instruments, NGO reports, academic texts, and media demonstrates good research relevance to the question.
The argument follows a logical progression from conceptual frameworks through case examples, culminating in conclusions that engage with EU responses.
Discussion presents a clear viewpoint on the conflict between LGBT-free zones and EU values, and partial critical evaluation of alternative interpretations is evident.
weaknesses: Analysis tends toward descriptive narration rather than systematic application of theoretical frameworks to evidence.
Conclusions are only partly supported; counter-arguments (sovereignty claims) are mentioned but not deeply examined.
Critical evaluation of enforcement mechanisms and the effectiveness of EU sanctions is limited and would benefit from deeper assessment.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Performance in Critical Thinking falls solidly within the upper end of the mid band (4â6). The student conducts good, relevant research and presents a reasoned argument with some evaluation, but analysis and evaluation lack the depth and rigour of the top band. A score of 6/12 fairly reflects this level of critical engagement.

strengths: Structure is clear and generally appropriate, with a logical sequence of introduction, conceptual framework, analysis, and conclusion.
Formal presentation elements (title page, table of contents, headers, footnotes, bibliography) are present and support readability.
weaknesses: Frequent typographical errors, inconsistent citation formatting, and embedded supervisor comments interrupt the flow and detract from polish.
Layout conventions are applied unevenly (e.g. figures placed in the main text, inconsistent heading hierarchy).
Redundancies (duplicate research question) and an overly dense narrative in places hinder smooth navigation and professional presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets basic IB requirements and maintains reader comprehension, but typographical issues, inconsistent formatting, and lapses in structural polish prevent a topâband score. A 3/4 accurately captures this competent but imperfect level of presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning:
â¢ **Argument is present and broadly logical,** moving from conceptual background to national and international responses and culminating in a conclusion that answers the question.
â¢ **Analysis leans toward narrative description.** While the student invokes liberalism, realism and queer theory, these frameworks are not systematically applied to dissect evidence; passages often list events rather than analyse causation or implications.
â¢ **Conclusions are partly supported**âEU sanctions linked to municipal reversalsâbut counter-arguments (e.g., sovereignty claims) are only briefly examined. This places the work at the lower end of the 4-6 âadequateâ analytical band.

â¢ **Substantial body of evidence collected**: legal documents, NGO reports, academic texts and media sources cover local, national and supranational levels, indicating diligence and variety.
â¢ **Relevance to the RQ is generally maintained,** especially through EU legal instruments and case examples of municipalities.
â¢ **However, depth of primary research is absent** and some sources (e.g., YouTube explainers) undermine academic quality. The research remains good rather than excellent, fitting securely within the 4-6 band at an upper-mid score.

â¢ **Logical macro-structure is evident** (Introduction, conceptual framework, national/international analysis, conclusion), and a table of contents is provided.
â¢ **Some structural weaknesses**: occasional repetition, digressions, and uneven section weighting (lengthy conceptual discussion versus brief methodology) hinder the smooth progression of the argument, though comprehension is still supported overall.
â¢ **Hence, the structure is clear and generally appropriate but not consistently well balanced or fully integrated, warranting a lower 3-4 band score.

â¢ **Shows clear familiarity with EU human-rights architecture and global-politics theories** (liberalism, realism, sovereignty, queer theory). Key legal provisions (Articles 13, 19, Charter Art 21) are correctly cited, and political context is outlined.
â¢ **Coherence is uneven.** Explanations of theories sometimes drift or remain superficial (e.g., brief, undeveloped mention of queer theory; limited depth on liberal/realist perspectives). Nonetheless, the narrative demonstrates a sound grasp of the topic overall.
â¢ **Use of sources is partly effective.** Authoritative legal documents are quoted, yet the argument often reverts to narrative reportage rather than analytical engagement, capping the strand at the mid band.

â¢ **Discussion presents a clear viewpoint** that LGBT-free zones conflict with EU values and evaluates EU responses (funding cuts, infringement procedures) as effective soft-power tools.
â¢ **Evaluation is partial.** Alternative interpretations (e.g., realist defence of sovereignty) are mentioned but not deeply explored; implications for future EU-Poland relations are hinted at rather than critically assessed.
â¢ **Structure of the discussion is sound but relies heavily on descriptive chronology.** The essay moves towards evaluation in the conclusion, yet the depth of critical reflection is moderate, justifying a mid-band score.

â¢ **Formal requirements largely met:** title page, table of contents, headers, footnotes and bibliography are present; word count is within limits.
â¢ **Minor inconsistencies** in citation formatting, occasional typographical errors and inclusion of a figure within the main body (contrary to EE guidelines) show that layout conventions are applied but not flawlessly.
â¢ Overall, the presentation supports readability yet falls short of the polished accuracy required for the top band.

â¢ **Terminology such as âsovereigntyâ, âsoft powerâ, âinterdependenceâ, âqueer theoryâ, âIGOsâ and âhuman rightsâ is generally used correctly,** supporting reader understanding.
â¢ **Consistency issues appear.** Occasional inaccuracies (e.g., âOrdo Lurisâ vs âOrdo Iurisâ; conflating soft power with sanctions) and some vague phrasing reduce precision.
â¢ **Overall command is solid but not flawless,** placing performance in the competent 3-4 band rather than the consistently accurate 5-6 band.

â¢ **Clear statement and contextualisation of the topic and research focus.** The opening pages state the theme (LGBT-free zones in Poland) and situate it within EU values, human rights and global politics, giving readers a solid overview of why the issue matters.
â¢ **Sufficient but not flawless explanation.** The student summarises key historical facts, identifies principal actors and outlines the stakes for Poland and the EU. Occasional vagueness (e.g. conflating Ordo Iuris with local authorities, or asserting trends without evidence) prevents the explanation from being fully comprehensive.
â¢ **Logical communication throughout.** Headings, sub-headings and a functional introduction help the reader follow the line of inquiry, but lapses in precision and editing (e.g. spelling of âOrdo Iurisâ, repeated references) indicate that communication, while adequate to good, falls short of the consistently precise, sophisticated standard required for the top band.

â¢ **Range of sources is broad** (EU legal texts, NGO reports, news media, academic articles), indicating purposeful collection of material.
â¢ **Limited explicit methodological reflection.** Apart from listing sources, the student offers almost no discussion of source selection criteria, source evaluation, or data-gathering strategy; primary data are absent and secondary sources include several popular media and YouTube clips of uneven academic weight.
â¢ **Relevance generally assured but selection not always discerning.** Despite some strong legal and academic references, the mixture with less reliable sources and lack of critical source appraisal keeps the work within a competent but not fully developed methodological band.

â¢ **Research question is explicitly stated** and guides the essay; the wording (âHow do the LGBT+ free zones â¦ correspond to the EUâs values and principles?â) shows intentional focus on a normative comparison.
â¢ **Partial focus and some drift.** Large sections recount Polish and EU events in narrative form without always linking back to the central âcorrespondenceâ criterion; discussion sometimes expands into tangential issues (e.g., Polish electoral politics) without clear tie-back.
â¢ **Scope could be sharper.** The question collapses several complex dimensions (legal compliance, political values, human-rights discourse) into one, and methodology is not explicitly aligned with sub-questions, leading to a research focus that is good but not âsharplyâ delimited.

â¢ **Evidence is mostly pertinent.** EU treaties, parliamentary resolutions and reputable NGO reports are used to substantiate claims about discrimination and sanctions.
â¢ **Some weaker inclusions.** Reliance on journalistic pieces, YouTube explainers and duplicated references dilutes overall rigour; source integration tends to be descriptive rather than critically interrogative.
â¢ **Application is generally appropriate**âsources are brought in to illustrate PolandâEU tensionsâbut selective use and occasional lack of synthesis prevent the consistently âclearâ and âappropriateâ deployment expected at the top band.

annotations.comment: The text conflates the roles of NGOs and local authorities in creating free-zones; clarify each actorâs responsibilities to enhance factual precision.
The conceptual section defines theories but never links them back to the correspondence research question; explicitly connect each theory to your analysis.
The use of EU legal texts and NGO reports to substantiate claims is appropriate and demonstrates a solid application of relevant source material.
The research question is duplicated on the title page and in the introduction; consider presenting it once in the introduction to improve focus and avoid redundancy.
The description of âCampaign Against Homophobiaâ is generic; link NGO activities to the research questionâs focus on normative correspondence.
The narrative flow is interrupted by multiple footnotes; integrate essential footnote content into the main text for cohesive argumentation.
The Table of Contents lists an Appendix entry but should exclude it here; appendices follow the bibliography, so remove it from the main TOC.
The EU action narrative is dense and chronological; condense into analytical commentary that links measures back to the research question focus.
Figure caption is informal and lengthy; revise to a concise academic caption (e.g., âFigure 1. Atlas of Hate map showing LGBT-free zones and regions rejecting measures.â).
The heading hierarchy is inconsistent (H1, H2, H2); standardise heading levels to support clear navigation and visual coherence.
A methodology section is listed in the TOC but not present; add a dedicated methodology section outlining source selection criteria and research approach.
A âMethodologyâ section is absent despite its TOC entry; include explicit explanation of research design, source selection, and analytical approach.
Bibliography entries mix styles (italics vs. plain, inconsistent author formatting); standardise to one citation style (e.g., MLA or APA) for professionalism.
Supervisor comments (e.g., [JH1]) remain in the main text; remove all tracked feedback to present a polished final draft.
This paragraph is lengthy and covers multiple concepts; break into focused subsections or shorter paragraphs to guide the reader effectively.
The introduction cites a YouTube video for a key ideological definition; replace with a peer-reviewed source to strengthen academic rigour and relevance.
Reliance on a YouTube source for âInterdependenceâ is insufficient; incorporate foundational IR texts (e.g., Keohane, Nye) to deepen conceptual rigour.
The discussion of sovereignty remains descriptive; analyse how sovereignty claims conflict or align with specific EU legal principles to support your argument.
Supervisor comments ([JH6]) are still embedded; remove editorial remarks to maintain professional presentation.
EU legal framework is summarised but lacks critical evaluation; assess how enforcement mechanisms influence municipal compliance with EU values.
Listing resolution provisions is mainly narrative; interpret these provisions in relation to EU anti-discrimination norms to deepen analytical insight.
Map and figure discussion remains descriptive; apply theoretical frameworks (liberalism/realism) to evaluate the impact of declarations on local dynamics.
The figure is referenced in the main body rather than an appendix; relocate all visual elements to the appendix to comply with EE formatting rules.
Conclusion summarises content without synthesising key findings; directly evaluate how free-zones correspond to EU values and principles to answer your question.
The Response section outlines actions but lacks evaluation of effectiveness; assess how sanctions influenced municipal reversals in substantive terms.
